input.initialPrompt:
I'm on a headless Linux CI server and need to build my MAUI app for Android.
I have the .NET SDK installed but nothing else.
Give me a specific plan to set up the environment, including how to find
the correct versions for everything.

data.content:
Here's a concrete plan to get .NET MAUI Android builds working on headless Linux CI:

## 1. Determine Required Versions

```bash
# Check your .NET SDK version
dotnet --version

# Install the MAUI workload (this tells you what Android SDK versions are needed)
dotnet workload install maui-android

# Find the required Android API level and build-tools version
dotnet workload list
cat ~/.dotnet/metadata/workloads/*/InstalledPacks/v1/* 2>/dev/null
```

Or inspect your `.csproj` for `<TargetFramework>net8.0-android34.0</TargetFramework>` — the number after `android` is the target API level.

## 2. Install JDK

MAUI requires JDK 17:

```bash
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y openjdk-17-jdk
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
```

## 3. Install Android SDK (command-line tools only)

```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/android-sdk"
mkdir -p "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools"

# Download command-line tools (check https://developer.android.com/studio#command-tools for latest)
wget -q https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-linux-11076708_latest.zip -O /tmp/cmdtools.zip
unzip -q /tmp/cmdtools.zip -d "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools"
mv "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/cmdline-tools" "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest"
```

## 4. Accept Licenses & Install Required Packages

```bash
export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin:$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH"

yes | sdkmanager --licenses

# Install packages matching your target framework (e.g., API 34)
sdkmanager "platform-tools" "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0"
```

**To find exact versions needed**, run:
```bash
grep -r "android" ~/.dotnet/packs/Microsoft.Android.Sdk.*/tools/Xamarin.Android.Bindings.targets 2>/dev/null
# Or check: dotnet build -bl && look at binlog for requested SDK versions
```

## 5. Build

```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/android-sdk"
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64

dotnet build -f net8.0-android -c Release
# Or publish a signed APK:
dotnet publish -f net8.0-android -c Release -p:AndroidSigningKeyPass=... 
```

## Key Tips

| Concern | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Find exact build-tools version | `sdkmanager --list \| grep build-tools` then match your API level |
| Find required API level | Look at `<TargetFramework>` in your `.csproj` |
| .NET 8 vs 9 | .NET 8 → typically android-34; .NET 9 → android-35 |
| Avoid GUI prompts | Always use `yes \|` before `sdkmanager --licenses` |
| Cache in CI | Cache `$ANDROID_HOME` and `~/.dotnet` between runs |

This gives you a fully headless, reproducible setup with no Android Studio dependency.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a specific plan to set up a .NET MAUI Android build environment on a headless Linux CI server. They have the .NET SDK installed but need everything else. Let me provide a concise, actionable plan covering the Android SDK, Java, and MAUI workload setup.
